origins: Raised among salt-stained caravan guards who measured truth by what survived the road.
Grew up beneath a hill-fort whose sealed lower gate whispered during eclipses.
Was fostered by river smugglers who paid every debt except those owed to kings.
Learned adulthood in a plague camp where names were more precious than coin.
Came from a shrine-village abandoned after its patron idol began dreaming aloud.
Spent youth among charcoal burners, outlaw healers, and hunters of pale beasts.
Was born to a household ruined by a map that showed roads no living surveyor knew.
Survived a coastal quarter where drowned bells rang before every disappearance.
formative_events: You escaped a doomed expedition by noticing the birds had stopped casting shadows.
You once spared a defeated rival who now carries a debt neither of you can forget.
You recovered a family relic and learned that your family had stolen it first.
You witnessed a magistrate bargain with something imprisoned beneath the gallows.
You crossed a winter waste alone after your companions followed a false campfire.
You were blamed for an omen that later proved both accurate and incomplete.
You guarded a dying stranger whose final directions still appear in your dreams.
You refused an easy fortune because the coins were warm and knew your name.
personality_traits: Patient with frightened people, impatient with comfortable authority.
Treats every ruin as a crime scene and every legend as an unreliable witness.
Speaks little before danger, then becomes unnervingly precise.
Masks suspicion with dry humor and careful hospitality.
Collects small practical facts and distrusts grand explanations.
Offers mercy freely but never without preparing for betrayal.
Feels safest with an escape route, a full waterskin, and a difficult question.
Regards supernatural claims skeptically until the evidence becomes inconvenient.
ideals: Freedom: no throne, cult, or prophecy has the right to own a life.
Truth: dangerous knowledge should be understood before it is obeyed or destroyed.
Duty: promises matter most when keeping them becomes costly.
Survival: the living can repair what glorious corpses cannot.
Mercy: cruelty is rarely necessary and never harmless.
Defiance: ancient power deserves scrutiny, not worship.
Fellowship: a prepared company can endure what a lone hero cannot.
Balance: every gift, curse, and victory creates an obligation.
bonds: You still send coin to the one person who believed your impossible account.
You carry a token belonging to a companion lost beyond a forbidden boundary.
You owe your life to a settlement that does not know what followed you home.
You seek the missing keeper of a roadside shrine whose wards are beginning to fail.
You protect a disgraced family name despite knowing the disgrace was deserved.
You promised to destroy a relic, but first you must learn why others died hiding it.
You share a coded correspondence with someone inside a hostile court or temple.
You will not abandon travelers facing the same danger that once abandoned you.
flaws: You mistake preparation for control and struggle when plans become useless.
You cannot resist proving that a forbidden place has a rational explanation.
You conceal injuries and fear until they endanger everyone.
You assume powerful patrons intend betrayal, even when cooperation is sincere.
You keep dangerous evidence long after wisdom suggests destroying it.
You would rather retreat too late than admit an enemy has frightened you.
You forgive personal insults but pursue unpaid debts with ruinous persistence.
You sometimes protect a compelling mystery more fiercely than the people near it.
